name: Incommons Bank, N.A.
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank operating in Texas with checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services. Member FDIC with locations in Waco, Mexia, and Fairfield.
description_long:
Incommons Bank, N.A. is a federally chartered national bank and FDIC member operating in Central Texas with a community-focused banking model. The bank emphasizes personalized service and local lending relationships, positioning itself as a neighbor-oriented financial institution rather than a large national chain.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business banking products. Personal services include checking accounts (Kasasa Cash, Kasasa Cash Back, and InControl varieties), savings accounts, CDs, IRAs, HSAs, personal loans, and personal debit cards. Business offerings encompass commercial checking accounts, business CDs, business debit cards, and various loan products. The loan portfolio spans personal loans, mortgages, home improvement financing, auto loans, construction loans, commercial real estate loans, equipment loans, agriculture loans, and business lines of credit.

Incommons Bank distinguishes itself through its Kasasa reward products—offering up to 6.00% APY on checking balances up to $20,000 and 3.00% APY on savings up to $50,000 with monthly qualification requirements. The bank emphasizes fee-free checking and surcharge-free ATM access. Their digital banking platform provides secure account access and fund transfers. The bank also offers merchant card services and maintains multiple physical locations across Texas.

As a regional community bank, Incommons Bank serves customers seeking local decision-making and personalized lending, though it operates in a limited geographic footprint. The high APY rates are conditioned on meeting specific qualification thresholds. Customer access is primarily limited to their Texas service areas through physical branches or digital channels.
services: Personal checking accounts (Kasasa Cash, Kasasa Cash Back, InControl, Interest Checking)
Personal savings accounts with competitive APY rates
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) for both personal and business customers
Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) and Health Savings Accounts (HSAs)
Personal loans with flexible terms and competitive rates
Home mortgage loans and home improvement loans
Business checking accounts with up to 1,000 free deposits monthly
Commercial real estate loans, equipment loans, and agriculture loans
Business lines of credit and business term loans
Digital banking platform for account management and fund transfers
Debit cards (personal and business) with surcharge-free ATM access
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Kasasa Cash checking offers 6.00% APY on balances up to $20,000 with monthly qualification
Kasasa Saver offers 3.00% APY on savings accounts up to $50,000
Comprehensive loan offerings including mortgages, construction, agriculture, and commercial real estate financing
Surcharge-free ATM access and fee-free checking options available
Local community-oriented lending with personalized service from neighborhood lenders
Digital banking platform for secure account management and fund transfers
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Texas locations (Waco, Mexia, Fairfield)
High APY rates on checking and savings require monthly qualification thresholds—rates not guaranteed for all balances
No online-only banking option; customers in non-service areas cannot access accounts
Website indicates customers need specific balance minimums and deposit activity to maintain advertised rates
best_for: Texas residents in Waco, Mexia, and Fairfield seeking community bank relationships with personalized service
Consumers wanting fee-free checking with high APY rates who can meet monthly qualification requirements
Small business owners needing local lending relationships and commercial banking services
Homebuyers seeking mortgage financing from local lenders with understanding of regional market conditions
diagnosis: Incommons Bank is best for Texas residents in their service areas seeking relationship-based community banking with competitive rates on checking and savings products. The main caveat is that advertised high APY rates (6.00% checking, 3.00% savings) require monthly qualification thresholds and are only guaranteed on specific balance tiers, making them inaccessible to all customers and all account balances.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
